opponent_lineup.source: We split the 2025 season series with the Patriots 1-1 — a 14-9 win on Aug 7 and a 7-14 loss in the Aug 22 rematch, so this one owes us nothing and settles nothing. They're a right-handed lineup with handedness on file, which means a pull-heavy, left-side attack — shade the left-center gap and make them prove they can go the other way. The one exception is lefty S. Mazzie, who flips the script and pulls to the RIGHT; slide the whole defense over when he digs in. Full at-bat-by-at-bat breakdown and per-hitter alignment calls are on the Patriots Scout page.

rules: ROAD GAME at Harry Lee Cole School, 26 Middleton Rd, Boxford — first pitch 6:30, be there by 5:30. It's the Patriots, who we split with 1-1 in 2025 (won 14-9, dropped the rematch 7-14). Eric Tailgate is on.

Twelve hands confirmed. OUT tonight: Andrew Amann (#21), Ian Konen (#14), Kyle Doherty, and Luke McArdle. John Flinn (#19) hasn't RSVP'd — if he rolls in, he takes first base and slots near the top of the order (bump everyone down a peg). With Amann out, Perry DiLibero slides to left-center and owns it all seven; Thacher Knight patrols left field wire to wire. The order is ranked off the 2025 body of work.
PITCHING: Eric Klatt opens on the mound, innings 1–5, then hands the ball to Joe McLean to close it out over the last two. Our only two arms, and both showed up.

Twelve players, ten spots — so two fielders rest each inning. Five go wire to wire: Marks (short), DiLibero (left-center), Knight (left), Sang (first), and Sorenson (catcher) — their spots have no backup with this crew. The other seven each take exactly two defensive breathers; nobody sits three times. Everyone hits all game; SIT means a defensive rest only. The grid shows where you are each inning.

grid.why: A .565 on-base with exactly one strikeout in all of 2025 — the man reaches base like it's a court order and hands the at-bat back to no one. Leadoff is just the paperwork catching up to the talent. Takes his two breathers early, then locks down right field the rest of the way.

Hit .524 with zero walks and zero strikeouts in 63 trips — he has never once considered taking a pitch, and the box score keeps agreeing with him. Bats second to keep the line moving. Roams the right-side outfield, then straps on the gear for two innings behind the plate when Sorenson shifts to third.

.473 with four triples — at this level a triple is two miracles in one swing: crush it AND agree to run the whole way. Bats third and mans second base for five innings, sure hands up the middle after the infield shift.

The franchise bat, batting cleanup where he belongs: 14 homers and 53 RBI in 2025, both team highs. With Amann off tonight, Perry also inherits left-center and plays all seven — the bat AND the glove carry a load.

The .652 slugging with three bombs and eight doubles is the real headline — boom-or-bust pop batting fifth to protect the cleanup spot. Our only true shortstop, so he mans the six-hole in the dirt all seven innings.
.476 with seven doubles of pure gap larceny, batting sixth. Tonight he's the horse: opens on the mound and goes five strong, then hands it to Joe and takes his rest. We feed him, we thank him.

.514 at the plate and zero errors in the field in 2025 — the rare man who's calm in the box and calmer at first base. Bats seventh and mans first all seven innings, where very little gets by him. (If Flinn shows, they'll share the bag.)

A perfect 1.000 fielding percentage at the hot corner and a quiet .469 in 2025. Our best third baseman owns the hot corner for five innings; when he takes his two breathers, Sorenson slides over from behind the plate to cover.

.368 with a sneaky .429 on-base, batting ninth to flip the order over. Saves his legs early, spells right field and second in the middle innings, then closes the game on the mound across the last two. A full evening's labor.
.313 and the ultimate utility man — backfills wherever the grid springs a leak. Tonight that's right-center for five innings, bracketed by his two rests. Square one up and climb the order.

No 2025 book on the bat yet, so the 11-hole is the audition booth — but the glove is already trusted. With Amann out and Perry in left-center, Knight patrols left field all seven, no backup behind him. Get on base and rewrite your line.

The other new bat auditioning from the cellar, but a rock behind the plate: catches five innings and slides to third for two when Watson rests — the C/3B flexibility that keeps the whole rotation legal. Bats twelfth tonight; his glove plays higher.
